playbook.intent: One-line tweeted hypothesis → Gumroad/Stripe-collected first paying customer in 21 days.
playbook.scope: Solo indie hacker takes a single tweeted hypothesis through a 3-week audience-driven build sprint: validate via DMs, ship an ugly first version, open paid checkout, and convert at least one tweet reader into a paying user.

success-criteria.criterion: Tweeted hypothesis with measurable engagement signal (≥20 likes, ≥3 DMs)
Working ugly-first-version deployed at a public URL
Paid checkout live (Stripe Payment Link or Gumroad)
At least one paid invoice from a Twitter-referred buyer

stage.name: Hypothesis
stage.intent: Public one-line hypothesis tweeted; signal collected.

tasks.task: Draft three single-tweet hypotheses framed as 'X for Y'
Pick the one that aligns with current audience interests
Post and pin; reply to every signal within 12 hours

outputs.output: One pinned tweet capturing the hypothesis

stage.decision-gate: Advance if ≥20 likes OR ≥3 qualified DMs within 48h. Re-frame and retweet variant if signal is below threshold. Kill the bet if 0 signal after 2 variants.

stage.name: Validate
stage.intent: Confirm 5+ prospects describe the pain and accept a paid concept.

tasks.task: Run mom-test style DMs with the 10 most-engaged repliers
Pitch a price (≥$20) inside the conversation
Capture verbatim quotes that justify the build

stage.decision-gate: Advance if ≥5 DMs confirm the pain and ≥3 verbally commit to paying. Pivot scope or price if signal is mixed. Kill if no one mentions paying after explicit price.

stage.name: Scope
stage.intent: Cut to a one-screen build that ships in 7 days.

tasks.task: Write a one-screen micro-MVP spec — one job, one input, one output
Write the kill list — every feature explicitly NOT shipping
Define the 'paid' moment — what triggers Stripe checkout

stage.decision-gate: Advance if scope fits on one page AND build is realistic in ≤7 days. Re-cut if estimate exceeds a week.

stage.name: Build
stage.intent: Ugly-first-version live, paid checkout wired in.

tasks.task: Implement only the validated job; skip auth if not needed
Wire Stripe Payment Link or Gumroad as the checkout
Push to a public URL by Day 14

outputs.output: Working checkout that issues a real invoice

stage.decision-gate: Advance when one stranger completes the flow end-to-end and pays. Iterate UI ugliness only when paid flow blocks itself.

stage.name: Launch
stage.intent: Convert hypothesis tweet readers into paying customers.

tasks.task: Drop a launch tweet thread linking to the live product
DM the 5 strongest Validate stage signals personally
Cross-post to IndieHackers and Reddit niche subs

stage.decision-gate: Advance to Review if ≥1 paid invoice in 7 days post-launch. Re-pitch on a different surface if 0 conversion despite traffic.

stage.name: Review
stage.intent: Decide continue / iterate / kill with a written rationale.

tasks.task: Run a weekly review with the Discovery and Launch evidence
Compute first-week unit economics (revenue, refund risk)

stage.decision-gate: Required output: a written verdict. No 'we'll see'.

extra.value: [Multi-product portfolio strategy — single-bet playbook, Hiring or contractor delegation — pure solo loop, 'Enterprise compliance, SOC2, or legal review']
[Active X/Twitter account with >100 followers (otherwise run audience growth first), Stripe or Gumroad account verified for payouts]
[Treating the playbook as a checklist instead of a decision tree, Skipping decision gates when intermediate progress feels good, Letting tools or platforms replace the founder's own judgement]

['Did each stage produce its named output or get explicitly skipped with a note?', 'Were decision gates respected or quietly bypassed?', 'Is the evidence trail strong enough to defend the verdict externally?']

{human: faion get-content tweet-to-launch-sprint-3-weeks --format md, agent: faion get-content tweet-to-launch-sprint-3-weeks --format context, json: faion get-content tweet-to-launch-sprint-3-weeks --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from brainstorm-2026-05-17, persona P2 (indie hacker)'}]
